codeReview.summary: The change improves test determinism and corrects one backward-direction assertion, but it introduces a monotonicity gap in production cursor advancement.

issues.description: Monotonic enforcement allows backward movement up to 1ms via `newUpperBound.timeIntervalSince(currentBound) >= -epsilon`. This violates the expected monotonic cursor contract (non-decreasing advancement) and can persist a strictly earlier cursor bound under small timestamp jitter.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `skillFeedback.followedProcedure=true`, but the transcript skeleton does not show required context reads from the indexing-efficiency skill (`AGENTS.md`, `.factory/library/architecture.md`, `.factory/library/user-testing.md`).
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for workerSessionId `e98c1da7-9163-4333-88cd-8ef0d36d8d46` shows reads of mission.md, validation-contract.md, validation-state.json, features.json, and test/store files, but no reads of AGENTS.md or the two required library docs before EndFeatureRun.

summary: Reviewed transcript skeleton and commit 991904f5c08dae62b3cebcfc0486bbb0a4e6b9d4 for fast-surfaces-fix-backfillscheduler-regressions. Marked fail due to a blocking monotonicity regression in BackfillCursorStore; also noted a skill-procedure evidence gap.
